policy.description: Blocking line-coverage ratchets. Update measured counts in the same reviewed change whenever scope or coverage advances; never lower a floor without an explicit, justified scope change.

tolerance.rationale: Two final clean local Node 22/V8 runs on 2026-07-16 each passed 1203 tests and identically measured the complete 2180-line core scope at 1842 covered lines (84.495%). Issue #318's final manifest-owned runtime, monolith retirement, route-generation ownership, and delayed/retried activation regressions expand the reviewed V8 scope by 31 instrumented lines and advance the high-water by 41 covered lines relative to the intermediate 1801/2149 measurement, raising line coverage from 83.806%. The existing one-line tolerance is unchanged and permits only the previously reproduced negligible instrumentation variance; coverage-baseline.test.js retains exact below-floor, above-baseline, scope-change, and broad-tolerance negative boundaries.

Two clean local .NET 10.0.9/Coverlet integration runs on 2026-07-16 each passed 1668 tests and measured the identical combined 27277-line assembly scope at 18743 and 18744 covered lines. Issue #318 adds the fail-closed manifest-owned client distribution catalog, build-generation resource validation and serving, deterministic embedded-resource contracts, and controller regressions for malformed, stale, incompatible, and valid inventories. Relative to the reviewed 18443/26889 baseline, the reviewed assembly scope grows by 388 instrumented lines and the high-water advances by 301 covered lines while line coverage rises from 68.589% to 68.717%. The existing two-line tolerance is unchanged and covers the reproduced one-line Coverlet variance; coverage-baseline.test.js retains the exact scope-drift, regression, stale-baseline, and broad-tolerance negative boundaries.
